plan: Step 1: Create receipt component CSS ‚Äî Create `src/styles/receipt.css` with styles for `.receipt` (card wrapper with receipt aesthetic: subtle border, clean spacing), `.receipt-title` (title area with bottom border separator, muted accent styling), `.receipt-row` (flexbox label/value pairs with dotted leader between label and value, alternating subtle backgrounds), and `.receipt-rows` (container for variable number of rows). Use existing design tokens from tokens.css. Include reduced-motion support and responsive adjustments.
Step 2: Import receipt CSS ‚Äî Add `@import './receipt.css';` to `src/styles/global.css` so the receipt styles are available globally, following the existing pattern used for card.css and button.css.

Step 3: Create receipt component TypeScript ‚Äî Create `src/ui/receipt.ts` exporting three pure functions: (1) `createReceiptRow(label: string, value: string): HTMLElement` ‚Äî returns a div.receipt-row with label span and value span, (2) `createReceiptTitle(title: string): HTMLElement` ‚Äî returns a header element with .receipt-title class, (3) `createReceipt(title: string, rows: Array<{label: string; value: string}>): HTMLElement` ‚Äî composes a full receipt card using createReceiptTitle and createReceiptRow, supporting variable number of rows. Export a `ReceiptRow` interface with `label` and `value` fields.

Step 4: Write unit tests ‚Äî Create `tests/us-011/receipt.test.ts` with jsdom environment. Test: (a) receipt card renders as a container element with correct class, (b) receipt title area renders with provided title text, (c) receipt row renders label/value pairs correctly, (d) receipt supports variable number of rows (0, 1, 5+ rows), (e) semantic HTML structure (headings, proper nesting), (f) CSS file exists and contains expected class selectors.
Step 5: Run validation ‚Äî Execute `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all code passes linting, type checking, and unit tests.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: createReceipt renders a container element with the `.receipt` class
Unit test: createReceiptTitle renders the title text within a heading element with `.receipt-title` class
Unit test: createReceiptRow renders both label and value text content correctly
Unit test: createReceipt with 0 rows renders title but no row elements
Unit test: createReceipt with 5+ rows renders all rows in order
Unit test: Verify receipt.css file exists and contains `.receipt`, `.receipt-title`, `.receipt-row` selectors
Unit test: Verify receipt.css is imported in global.css
Manual check: Visual inspection in browser at mobile and desktop viewports
risks: Styling may need iteration to achieve the financial-report / receipt aesthetic described in CLAUDE.md ‚Äî the dotted leader pattern between label and value may need cross-browser testing
No existing UI component pattern to follow since src/ui/ is currently empty ‚Äî this sets the precedent for all future components
